user_query:
I’m trying to choose a new front bike light and want a proper, no-nonsense verdict based on my rides and preferences, plus a cheeky note to my mates. Please do the following using only the local files in input/:

1) Extract specs from input/products/light_a.html and input/products/light_b.md into a single CSV at output/extracted/lights.csv with columns exactly: model, lumens (integer), runtime_hours_range (e.g., "2-8"), weight_g (integer), waterproof_rating, warranty_years (integer), price_gbp (numeric without the £), charging, modes, source_file. Parse numbers from text (no currency symbols) and keep waterproof_rating as the IP code string. If modes are mentioned, include a concise comma-separated list; otherwise leave blank.

2) Read input/rides/commute_log.csv and summarise to output/analysis/commute_summary.json with keys: total_rides, after_dark_rides, rain_rides, visibility_incident_rides, notes. Rules:
- after_dark_rides = count of rides where time_of_day != "day" (case-insensitive)
- rain_rides = count where conditions contains "rain" (case-insensitive)
- visibility_incident_rides = count of rides where issues includes either "invisible" or "pulled out" (case-insensitive)
- notes = a list of unique non-empty issues strings as they appear (trimmed)

3) Read input/notes/preferences.txt and use it with (1) and (2) to write a plain-text critique and recommendation at output/review/light_recommendation.txt. Requirements:
- Compare the two lights using the extracted CSV (don’t hardcode numbers)
- Explicitly state whether each model meets the budget and weight preference, and any trade-offs (brightness, waterproof rating, warranty)
- Reference the commute summary by citing the computed counts (e.g., after_dark_rides, rain_rides) to justify the pick
- End with a single-line verdict: "Verdict: <MODEL_NAME>"

4) Draft a short message for my cycling WhatsApp group at output/messages/whatsapp_message.txt (<=120 words). Tone: casual UK cyclist with light banter. Include the chosen model and one concrete reason tied to the commute summary (e.g., number of after-dark or rainy rides). No links.

Make sure all four outputs are created and consistent with the inputs. Cheers! All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
